sub_tasks.requirements: Method and code fidelity to the paper

train.py implements the sigmoid gap gate g_t = σ(β · Δ_t) where Δ_t is the detached teacher-student log-probability gap on the student-sampled token (Δ_t = log π_teacher(y_t|...) − log π_student(y_t|...)), with β=10 as specified in Section 3 (Hyperparameters) and Section 2.3.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The code applies a stop-gradient (detach) to both π_teacher and the gate g_t so gradients flow exclusively through the student log-probability, matching Proposition 1 and Section 2.3 (sg[·] operator).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The overall loss is implemented as L_total = L_GRPO + λ · L_OPSD with λ=0.1 (Section 3 Hyperparameters), where L_GRPO is the standard clipped GRPO objective with group-relative advantages and L_OPSD is the token-level gated reverse-KL surrogate per Section 2.2–2.3.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: GRPO baseline (Algorithm 2) is implemented with the clipped surrogate objective and group-relative advantages from Equation 2, including the importance sampling ratio and reference-policy KL penalty α_KL as listed in Table 3 of Appendix A.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The three alternative gating strategies from Section 2.3 are implemented and selectable: entropy gating σ(β·H_t), gap gating σ(β·Δ_t), and soft-OR gating σ(β·H_t)+σ(β·Δ_t)−σ(β·H_t)·σ(β·Δ_t), with gap gating as the default.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The hybrid baselines GRPO+OPSD (Algorithm 5), Skill-SD (Algorithm 4), RLSD (Algorithm 6) and standalone OPSD (Algorithm 3) are re-implemented per Appendix A, all using the same Qwen backbone as SDAR so Table 1 comparisons are apples-to-apples.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Data and preprocessing fidelity
ALFWorld data uses the training split from GiGPO (Feng et al. 2025) covering the six task categories Pick, Look, Clean, Heat, Cool, Pick2 with 3,827 task instances, as stated in Section 3.

Search-QA training data is drawn from NQ and HotpotQA (in-domain) with TriviaQA, PopQA, 2Wiki, MuSiQue and Bamboogle held out for out-of-domain evaluation, using E5 (Wang et al. 2022) as the retriever, matching the Search-R1 setup (Section 3).
WebShop uses 1000 training tasks and 128 fixed validation tasks aligned with Feng et al. 2025 (Section 3).

Skill library is the SkillBank from SkillRL (Xia et al. 2026) for all three environments, and the four retrieval strategies (UCB, Keyword Matching, Full Retrieval, Random Retrieval) are implemented per Section 2.1, with KM as the default skill retrieval strategy (Table 3).
Experiment execution and reproducibility
Training is executed for 150 steps on the Qwen2.5-Instruct and Qwen3-Instruct backbones used in the paper (Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, Qwen3-1.7B-Instruct), as specified in Section 3.
ALFWorld training uses batch size 16 tasks × 8 rollouts per prompt with maximum prompt length 2,048 tokens (Section 3).
Search-QA training uses batch size 128 tasks with maximum prompt length 4,096 tokens (Section 3).
WebShop training uses batch size 16 tasks × 8 rollouts per prompt with maximum prompt length 4,096 tokens (Section 3).

Training hardware/runtime configuration matches the paper's '8 H800 GPUs' setup or a documented equivalent multi-GPU configuration, and the run executes the complete training-to-evaluation pipeline (Section 3).
Evaluation protocol and metric correctness

At inference time SDAR is evaluated without retrieved skills (the {skill_context} prompt slot is left empty per Appendix C), while Skill-GRPO* and other *-marked baselines are evaluated with skill context, matching the * convention in Table 1.
Evaluation prompt templates for ALFWorld, Search-QA, and WebShop follow the exact templates in Figures 15–17 of Appendix C, with {skill_context} populated during training and empty during inference.
Training-dynamics diagnostics from Figures 10–14 are logged per run: Gate Active Ratio (fraction where g_t>0.5), Gate Mean, OPSD Loss, Teacher-Student Gap (mean Δ_t), and Reward Curve.
Result match versus the paper's reported targets

On Qwen2.5-3B with ALFWorld, the reproduced SDAR success rate is within a small tolerance of the paper's 84.4% and shows the reported +9.4% improvement over GRPO (75.0% → 84.4%) reported in Table 1 and Section 3.2.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On Qwen2.5-3B with Search-QA, the reproduced SDAR accuracy reflects the reported +7.0% improvement over GRPO (Table 1, Section 3.2).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On WebShop, the reproduced SDAR WebShop-Acc reflects the reported +10.2% gain on 7B and +4.7% gain on 3B over GRPO (Section 1, Section 3.2).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On Qwen3-1.7B with ALFWorld, the reproduced SDAR success rate approaches 53.9% and exceeds RLSD (42.2%), GRPO+OPSD (32.0%), and GRPO (46.1%) as reported in Table 1 and Section 3.2.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduction confirms qualitative findings: GRPO+OPSD collapses on Qwen3-1.7B (≈32.0 vs GRPO 46.1), standalone OPSD collapses on Search-QA (near zero), and SDAR avoids these instabilities as shown in Figures 1–2 and Section 3.2.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Artifact completeness and provenance
Repository contains a runnable train.py / training entrypoint plus per-environment configs for ALFWorld, WebShop and Search-QA, along with documented commands to reproduce each row of Table 1.
Hyperparameter file or config records the exact Table 3 values (β=10, λ=0.1, group size G, PPO clip ratio ε, learning rate η, α_KL, skill retrieval strategy = KM) for SDAR and each baseline.

Logs and checkpoints include the five diagnostic curves from Figures 10–14 (gate active ratio, gate mean, OPSD loss, teacher-student gap, reward) and the final per-task metrics that populate Table 1, with provenance traceable to the original SDAR repo (https://github.com/ZJU-REAL/SDAR).
